essence: Monitors and analyses water quality to protect public health, ecosystems, and regulatory compliance.

identity.title: Water Quality Technician
identity.summary: Collects water samples, operates testing equipment, analyses contamination levels, and supports environmental protection and regulatory compliance.

salary.notes: Technicians working in advanced labs, utilities, or environmental agencies can exceed £52k-£65k+.

qualifications: Training in environmental science or water‑quality monitoring
Knowledge of water systems, contamination risks, and sampling methods
Ability to operate and maintain testing equipment
Understanding of environmental legislation and reporting standards

lifestyle.description: Water quality technicians work for water companies, environmental agencies, laboratories, and local authorities. They collect samples from rivers, reservoirs, treatment plants, and distribution networks, analysing data to protect public health and ecosystems. The role suits individuals who enjoy fieldwork, scientific testing, and contributing to environmental protection.

habits: Collects water samples from various sites
Works with scientists, regulators, and utilities
